# Homebrew cask — generated by GoReleaser

There is no hand-maintained formula here anymore (the previous `multiai.rb`
carried `PLACEHOLDER_*_SHA256` checksums, audit v0.2.1 finding #4).

A Homebrew **cask** is generated at release time by GoReleaser (see the
`homebrew_casks` section of `multiai-go/.goreleaser.yaml` — the old `brews`
formula config is deprecated since GoReleaser 2.10) with the real SHA256 of
each released archive. On every `v*` tag it lands in:

```
multiai-go/dist/homebrew/Casks/multiai.rb
```

The generated cask includes a post-install hook that removes the macOS
quarantine attribute, because the binary is not Apple-signed/notarized
(0 EUR signing plan: sha256 checksums + Cosign keyless instead).

## Publication

`skip_upload: true` until the tap exists. To publish:

1. Manual (first releases): copy the generated `multiai.rb` from the release
   run's `dist/` into a `lrochetta/homebrew-tap` repository under `Casks/`.
2. Automated: create the tap repo, add a `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN` repo secret
   (a PAT with write access to the tap — the default `GITHUB_TOKEN` cannot
   push to another repository), reference it via
   `repository.token: "{{ .Env.TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}"` and set
   `skip_upload: false` in `.goreleaser.yaml`.

Users then install with:

```sh
brew install --cask lrochetta/tap/multiai
```
